key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $0.78, down 45.1% YoY from $1.42
Operating profit of $728.0 million, down 32.5% YoY from $1,077.9 million
Restructuring, transformation, and impairment costs of $122.1 million, up significantly from $1.2 million YoY
qoq_changes: Net sales increased sequentially to $4,860.8 million in Q2 from an implied $4,517.5 million in Q1
Net earnings decreased sequentially to $413.0 million in Q2 from an implied $1,204.2 million in Q1, as Q1 benefited from a $1.05 billion divestiture gain
new_risks: Elevated restructuring and transformation costs are severely pressuring operating margins
Joint venture performance deteriorated sharply, swinging to an after-tax loss of $59.6 million from a $30.0 million gain YoY
management_tone: Cautious, as the company navigates a 7.2% YoY decline in net sales and significant margin pressures from restructuring efforts and joint venture losses.
analyst_note: General Mills is in the midst of a major portfolio transformation, evidenced by a massive $1.05 billion YTD divestiture gain and elevated Q2 restructuring costs. While YoY top-line and bottom-line metrics are weak, sequential revenue growth suggests core operations are stabilizing as the company executes its strategic realignment.
